registration.name: foldprobe-calibration
objects: GPT-2 h.0.mlp.c_fc.weight (known-loud, recorded 12.7x-class)
GPT-2 wte.weight (known-loud, recorded 79.3x-class flat / 67.6x rowblock)
Qwen3.6-27B blk.0.ffn_gate.weight (known-quiet, recorded ~1x)
He-init matched-shape matrix (negative control)
registration.statistic: battery C(k) at registered fractions + rowblock median margin
registration.verdict_rule: instrument FAITHFUL iff loud objects reproduce their recorded margin class, quiet object reads ~1x, He-init sits at null (no fraction beyond both nulls), and every bit-reversal self-test passes
registration.margin_clause: loud reproduction = within a factor of 1.5 of the recorded margin; quiet = flat-window margin < 2x; He-init = 0/3 fractions beyond nulls

registration.name: ledger-backfill
objects: llm_presence_results.txt
rung2f_results.txt
rung2f_rowblock_results.txt
rung2g_results.txt
rung2h_results.txt
kimi_results.txt
registration.statistic: verbatim margins/medians as committed in the rung record
registration.verdict_rule: a row is ingested iff its line parses against the file's own committed format; unparsed verdict lines are counted and reported, never silently dropped
registration.margin_clause: not applicable -- no new measurement is made; recorded margins are carried verbatim

registration.name: moe-expert-remeasure
objects: Qwen3-235B, Qwen3-480B, Kimi-K2.6-1T: ffn gate/up expert tensors at early/mid/late depth, experts sampled (first, middle, last); plus shared-expert (shexp) 2D tensors where present
registration.statistic: rowblock median margin (corrected 2f instrument) per expert slice
registration.verdict_rule: these rows REPLACE the 2f non-measurements (0.00x instrument failures); expert fingerprint = median margin > 2x on any expert slice; quiet reading routes to the coordinate hunt, per the epistemic correction
registration.margin_clause: wake threshold fixed at 2x (the 2g bar); readings between 1x and 2x are recorded as quiet-at-these-coordinates
registration.certification: bytewise expert slicing certified against whole-tensor dequant on the smallest expert tensor before any probe

registration.name: moe-expert-remeasure
objects: Qwen3-235B, Qwen3-480B, Kimi-K2.6-1T: ffn gate/up expert tensors at early/mid/late depth, experts sampled (first, middle, last); plus shared-expert (shexp) 2D tensors where present
registration.statistic: rowblock median margin (corrected 2f instrument) per expert slice
registration.verdict_rule: these rows REPLACE the 2f non-measurements (0.00x instrument failures); expert fingerprint = median margin > 2x on any expert slice; quiet reading routes to the coordinate hunt, per the epistemic correction
registration.margin_clause: wake threshold fixed at 2x (the 2g bar); readings between 1x and 2x are recorded as quiet-at-these-coordinates
registration.certification: bytewise expert slicing certified against whole-tensor dequant on the smallest expert tensor before any probe

registration.name: data-decode
objects: calibration twin (5b/5d recipe, seed 1, trained on the arena corpus)
GPT-2 wte + BPE vocab (HF cache snapshot)
Ollama llama3.2:1b (orbit-echo)
registration.statistic: P1 Spearman(kinship, cosine) with 5 shuffled-assignment nulls; P2 unigram-weighted mean CE of corpus bigrams under the model readout; P3 greedy echo word-length with shuffled-stem null
registration.verdict_rule: P1/P2 calibration PASSES iff the twin ranks its own training corpus first (above the word-shuffled copy and decoys); P3 calibration PASSES iff private-corpus stems read <= null+2 words while any public-domain reference echoes beyond it
registration.margin_clause: P1 provenance claim requires rho > 1.5x the shuffled-null max; P3 memorization claim requires echo >= 8 words with null < 3

registration.name: reasoning-decode
objects: GPT-2 (HF cache): MLP pre-activations + attention maps on 8 registered prompts
qwen3:8b via Ollama: <think> reasoning spans vs answer spans
NAMED DEPENDENCY: R1-Distill-32B vs qwen2.5-coder-32b activation run requires an activation-exposing GGUF runtime; registered as this instrument's next object
registration.statistic: P1 battery margin on concatenated activation vectors per layer; P2 mean TV distance of rank-sorted attention rows from the dyadic cascade vs uniform-TV and shuffled-mass null; P3 branching factor and repetition mass at depth 6, reasoning vs answer spans, with span-shuffled null
registration.verdict_rule: P1: activations loud (margin > 2x) in layers whose weights are loud = the computation carries the law; P2: cascade closer than uniform on median row = trained attention leans toward the fold's selection law; P3: reasoning-vs-answer signature gap beyond the shuffled null = reasoning is counted structure
registration.margin_clause: P1 wake bar 2x (the 2g bar); P2 claim requires median TV(cascade) < TV(uniform) AND < shuffled null; P3 gap must exceed the null gap by 1.5x

registration.name: twin-factory-6ab
objects: tiny twins (recorded 4L/128d recipe, seed 1), one per ingredient: baseline, optimizer, wdecay, lrsched, dropout, dataorder, rlpass; loud-class tensors (ff expansion + wte) + gradient accumulations
registration.statistic: locked battery margin at checkpoints 2^k, k=0..11, on weights and on the 8-batch gradient accumulation
registration.verdict_rule: WHAT: ingredient whose deposition curve departs from the baseline curve beyond the seed band; WHEN: first k with margin > wake bar; gradient stream louder earlier than weights = the law arrives through the updates
registration.margin_clause: wake bar 2x (the 2g bar); curve departure requires >= 2 consecutive checkpoints outside the baseline's min/max band

registration.name: capability-map
objects: GPT-2 (HF cache), tensor classes: wte, c_fc early/mid/late
16 registered prompts; mmlu_probe.json question texts grouped by subject as the domain axes
registration.statistic: mean next-token KL + top-1 agreement vs unmodified; per-domain mean NLL delta; arms ablate-loud vs ablate-random at k = n/64, n/16
registration.verdict_rule: ATTRIBUTION: a (class, domain) pair where loud-ablation's NLL delta exceeds random-ablation's at both budgets = those loud coefficients carry that capability
registration.margin_clause: attribution requires loud-delta > 1.5x random-delta at both budgets; calibration (k=0) must read KL=0, agreement=1 exactly

16 registered prompts; mmlu_probe.json question texts grouped by subject as the domain axes

registration.name: coordinate-hunt-round2
objects: Qwen3.6-27B: token_embd, blk.0 attn_q, blk.0 ffn_gate
Kimi-K2.6-1T: token_embd, blk.5 attn_q (shard-resolved), blk.5 ffn_gate_shexp
CONTROL: GPT-2 h.0.mlp.c_fc (must stay awake under F2 maps)
registration.statistic: concentration margin vs 3 shuffle-nulls at the registered fractions, per (index map, spectral basis) pair; bases: Walsh (the fold's), DCT-II ortho, Haar ortho
registration.verdict_rule: WAKE = margin > 2x under any (map, basis) pair; a woken tensor names the coordinates its recipe writes in; all quiet = these coordinates excluded, hunt continues
registration.margin_clause: wake bar fixed at 2x before any spectrum (the 2g bar); control must exceed 2x under identity or the run is void

registration.name: basis-atlas-7b
objects: one representative tensor per class (embedding / attn-Q / ffn-expansion / ffn-contraction) per on-drive model, expert-0 slices for MoE tensors
registration.statistic: paired margin vs 3 seeded shuffle-nulls in Walsh, DCT-II (ortho), Haar (ortho); same permutations across bases
registration.verdict_rule: two-basis law SUPPORTED iff woken embeddings are DCT-loudest in a majority AND woken expansions are Walsh-loudest in a majority; any other pattern recorded as the finding
registration.margin_clause: wake bar 2x; basis preference read only on cells with at least one basis > 2x
registration.followon: slant-family arm registered, to be added to foldprobe as a fourth comparator before atlas round 2

registration.name: checkpoint-telescope-7a
objects: EleutherAI/pythia-70m public checkpoints at steps [0, 1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 32, 64, 128, 256, 512, 1000, 4000, 16000, 64000, 143000]: embed_in + mlp.dense_h_to_4h at layers 0/2/5 (the loud class)
registration.statistic: locked battery margin (3 shuffle-nulls) + DCT/Haar comparator margins per tensor per checkpoint
registration.verdict_rule: the deposition curve margin(step) is the finding; step-0 must sit at null (built-in negative control) or the run is void; comparator curves answer whether DCT-loudness of embeddings emerges with training
registration.margin_clause: wake bar 2x (the standing bar); step-0 control null = no fraction beyond both nulls on any tensor

registration.name: basis-atlas-7b-round2-slant
objects: one representative tensor per class (embedding / attn-Q / ffn-expansion / ffn-contraction) per on-drive model, expert-0 slices for MoE tensors
registration.statistic: paired margin vs 3 seeded shuffle-nulls in Walsh, DCT-II (ortho), Haar (ortho); same permutations across bases
registration.verdict_rule: two-basis law SUPPORTED iff woken embeddings are DCT-loudest in a majority AND woken expansions are Walsh-loudest in a majority; any other pattern recorded as the finding
registration.margin_clause: wake bar 2x; basis preference read only on cells with at least one basis > 2x
registration.followon: round 2: the slant arm live (verified: ramp 1-sparse, energy exact)

registration.name: checkpoint-telescope-7a-pythia-410m
objects: EleutherAI/pythia-410m public checkpoints at steps [0, 256, 512, 1000, 2000, 4000, 8000, 16000, 64000, 143000]: embed_in + mlp.dense_h_to_4h at layers (0, 11, 23) (the loud class)
registration.statistic: locked battery margin (3 shuffle-nulls) + DCT/Haar/slant comparator margins per tensor per checkpoint
registration.verdict_rule: the deposition curve margin(step) is the finding; step-0 must sit at null (built-in negative control) or the run is void; across ladder rungs: does the deposition peak step move with model scale, and by what law?
registration.margin_clause: wake bar 2x (the standing bar); step-0 control null = no fraction beyond both nulls on any tensor

registration.name: checkpoint-telescope-7a-pythia-1.4b
objects: EleutherAI/pythia-1.4b public checkpoints at steps [0, 256, 512, 1000, 2000, 4000, 8000, 16000, 64000, 143000]: embed_in + mlp.dense_h_to_4h at layers (0, 11, 23) (the loud class)
registration.statistic: locked battery margin (3 shuffle-nulls) + DCT/Haar/slant comparator margins per tensor per checkpoint
registration.verdict_rule: the deposition curve margin(step) is the finding; step-0 must sit at null (built-in negative control) or the run is void; across ladder rungs: does the deposition peak step move with model scale, and by what law?
registration.margin_clause: wake bar 2x (the standing bar); step-0 control null = no fraction beyond both nulls on any tensor

registration.name: telescope-410m-step0-control
objects: pythia-410m step0 (pure init): embed_in + dense_h_to_4h layers 0/11/23
registration.statistic: FULL battery (5 shuffle-nulls + Gaussian) per tensor -- the flagged 3-null reading investigated at the registered strength
registration.verdict_rule: control CLEAN iff no tensor has any fraction beyond both nulls at the 5-null battery; a persisting beyond-null read on pure init voids the 410m rung's curve pending diagnosis
registration.margin_clause: clean = 0/3 fractions beyond on every tensor (standing)

registration.name: telescope-410m-step0-control-corrected
objects: pythia-410m step0, full 5-null battery (run a62dbf47694b7bd7)
registration.statistic: the campaign's own tensor-level structure criterion: a tensor shows structure iff ALL THREE fractions beat both nulls
registration.verdict_rule: control CLEAN iff zero tensors pass at 3/3. The earlier 0-beyond-anywhere criterion was over-strict for exchangeable nulls (pure init is exchangeable with its own shuffles, so single-fraction edge-outs are the null's expected behaviour).
registration.margin_clause: structure = 3/3 fractions, the presence suite's standing rule

registration.name: checkpoint-telescope-7a-pythia-1.4b-onset
objects: EleutherAI/pythia-1.4b public checkpoints at steps [32, 64, 128, 256, 512]: embed_in + mlp.dense_h_to_4h at layers (0, 11, 23) (the loud class)
registration.statistic: locked battery margin (3 shuffle-nulls) + DCT/Haar/slant comparator margins per tensor per checkpoint
registration.verdict_rule: the deposition curve margin(step) is the finding; step-0 must sit at null (built-in negative control) or the run is void; across ladder rungs: does the deposition peak step move with model scale, and by what law?
registration.margin_clause: wake bar 2x (the standing bar); step-0 control null = no fraction beyond both nulls on any tensor

registration.name: forced-band-test
objects: GPT-2 (HF cache): wte + all 12 c_fc, per-row ablation at the FORCED band k = 32 (Step 311) vs 32 random per row; row window = largest power-of-two prefix (instrument-class)
registration.statistic: mean next-token KL + top-1 agreement vs unmodified, registered 16-prompt set; k=0 calibration must be exact
registration.verdict_rule: the forced band carries the function iff band-KL > 10x random-KL and band agreement < 1/2 (the lock) with random agreement >= 1/2; recorded whichever way
registration.margin_clause: 10x KL ratio and the 1/2 lock, fixed before any run; the band size 32 is FORCED (b^cover(c^3)), not registered here

registration.name: forced-band-retention
objects: GPT-2 wte + all 12 c_fc: per-row RETENTION at the forced band (keep top-32 |Walsh| per row, zero the rest) vs keep-32-random per row (seeded); the 5c construction at full-model scale
registration.statistic: mean next-token KL + top-1 agreement vs unmodified, the registered 16-prompt set; k=0 calibration exact
registration.verdict_rule: the forced band carries the function iff KL(keep-random) > 10x KL(keep-band) AND keep-band agreement > keep-random agreement with keep-random below the 1/2 lock; recorded whichever way (the ablation arm saturated at this per-row budget and is recorded as rule-not-met, fa70888a85a52d3b)
registration.margin_clause: 10x KL ratio and the 1/2 lock, fixed before this run; band = 32, forced (Step 311)

registration.name: role-census
objects: the committed basis-atlas round-2 rows (11 public models x 4 tensor classes, slant arm included) from results.jsonl
registration.statistic: per (model, class): ROLE (architectural, forced) x FAMILY (recorded loudest basis: dct=colour; walsh/slant=binary; not-woken=quiet), cross-tabulated and matched to the forced role->family assignment (geometry->colour, selection->binary, performer->quiet)
registration.verdict_rule: family-follows-role SUPPORTED on the recorded reading iff woken geometry stores are colour in a majority AND all performer stores are quiet; selection->binary reported as its own count (the S316 value-signature re-read is the refinement where concentration is muddy). Recorded either way
registration.margin_clause: wake threshold 2.0x fixed; majority = strictly more than half of woken stores in the class; slant counted with walsh as the binary (dyadic) family, dct as colour

registration.name: deposition-fitter
objects: the committed checkpoint-telescope embedding rows (pythia 70m/410m/1.4b) from results.jsonl
registration.statistic: per scale: peak, plateau (last-two mean), and over the post-peak decay segment the linear fit of log2(margin - plateau) vs dyadic checkpoint index -- R^2, slope (= Measured timescale), point count
registration.verdict_rule: forced dyadic 2^-k form CONFIRMED for a scale iff >= 3 decay points AND R^2 >= 0.85 (geometric, no logistic inflection); < 3 decay points = DATA GAP (awaiting the finer-checkpoint run), not a verdict against the form
registration.margin_clause: R^2 >= 0.85 and >= 3 decay points, fixed before the run; the dyadic FORM is forced (Step 319), only the timescale is fit
